message: A file under the engine's surface is present in your working copy but git is not tracking it — most often a duplicate a file-sync tool (iCloud, Dropbox, OneDrive) left behind, with a name like `something 2.py`. The shared checks run on a clean copy of the repository and never see it, so it can cause a failure that only happens on your machine, and regenerating the engine's map of itself would pull it in. If a file-sync tool created it, delete it; if it is a new engine file you meant to add, commit it. This is a warning only and never blocks a merge.
